text:
*[Bug 19015] R segmentation fault in Windows with empty -e argument*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19015>

--- Comment #3 from Trevor Davis (<mailto:trevor.l.davis@gmail.com|trevor.l.davis@gmail.com>) ---
Created attachment 3713
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3713&action=edit>
Bill Denney's original patch reformatted as an svn patch

Comments contained from Bill Denney's original patch

> Fix: R_Suicide segfault when called before callback pointers are initialized
>
> On Windows, several callback dispatch functions (R_Suicide, R_ShowMessage,
R_CleanUp) unconditionally dereference function pointers (ptr_Suicide,
ptr_ShowMessage, ptr_CleanUp) that are NULL until R_SetParams() runs.
R_SetParams() is called near the end of cmdlineoptions() (line 1352), but
R_Suicide() is called during option parsing (e.g. line 1293 for an empty -e
expression), causing a NULL function pointer dereference
(STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION / 0xC0000005).
>
> The same latent bug exists on Unix: ptr_R_Suicide, ptr_R_ShowMessage, and ptr_R_CleanUp are set in Rf_initialize_R() (lines 313-321), but R_Suicide is called at line 292 (Solaris thr_stksegment failure) before initialization. The Unix code already uses NULL guards in some dispatch functions (R_WriteConsole and R_WriteConsoleEx at lines 77-78), but not in R_Suicide, R_ShowMessage, or R_CleanUp.
>
> The fix adds NULL guards with stderr fallbacks to all affected dispatch functions on both platforms, following the pattern already established by R_WriteConsole on Unix.

elements.text: [Bug 19015] R segmentation fault in Windows with empty -e argument

Bill Denney's original patch reformatted as an svn patch

Comments contained from Bill Denney's original patch

Fix: R_Suicide segfault when called before callback pointers are initialized

 On Windows, several callback dispatch functions (R_Suicide, R_ShowMessage,

R_CleanUp) unconditionally dereference function pointers (ptr_Suicide,
ptr_ShowMessage, ptr_CleanUp) that are NULL until R_SetParams() runs.
R_SetParams() is called near the end of cmdlineoptions() (line 1352), but
R_Suicide() is called during option parsing (e.g. line 1293 for an empty -e
expression), causing a NULL function pointer dereference
(STATUS_ACCESS_VIOLATION / 0xC0000005).
>

The same latent bug exists on Unix: ptr_R_Suicide, ptr_R_ShowMessage, and ptr_R_CleanUp are set in Rf_initialize_R() (lines 313-321), but R_Suicide is called at line 292 (Solaris thr_stksegment failure) before initialization. The Unix code already uses NULL guards in some dispatch functions (R_WriteConsole and R_WriteConsoleEx at lines 77-78), but not in R_Suicide, R_ShowMessage, or R_CleanUp.

 The fix adds NULL guards with stderr fallbacks to all affected dispatch functions on both platforms, following the pattern already established by R_WriteConsole on Unix.

Update of the patch

I updated the patch, dropping one code block that in my opinion wasn't good. 
I think some of the changes have their merit to make it easier for new readers. 
For example, it replaces some "magic numbers" with the output of the model
being explained. 

But this is not a big deal if the report is closed without further action.

text:
*[Bug 19079] New: S4 packages with group methods error with "subscript out of bounds" under unloadNamespace with Imports: methods*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19079>

            Bug ID: 19079
           Summary: S4 packages with group methods error with "subscript
                    out of bounds" under unloadNamespace with Imports:
                    methods
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: All
                OS: All
            Status: NEW
          Severity: minor
          Priority: P5
         Component: S4methods
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:michaelchirico4@gmail.com|michaelchirico4@gmail.com>

Created attachment 3716
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3716&action=edit>
Prevent uncaching "other" packages' generics

Similar to Bug #18540, I found that the CRAN package {spam} fails to unload
because it uses `Imports: methods` instead of `Depends: methods`:

```
R_DEFAULT_PACKAGES=NULL Rscript -e "loadNamespace('spam');
unloadNamespace('spam')"
# <environment: namespace:spam>
# Error in generics[[i]] : subscript out of bounds
# Calls: unloadNamespace ... .updateMethodsInTable -> .updateMethodsInTable ->
.checkGroupSigLength
# Execution halted
```

As discussed there and added in r84688 to WRE, as of now it's generally
encouraged to just use `Depends: methods` for S4 packages to work around such
issues.

Nevertheless, the bug is quite hard to make sense of, so a fix would be nice.

I deployed a friendly LLM (Gemini) to diagnose and fix the issue, and it wrote
a very simple fix, as attached here with a corresponding regression test. Its
internal reasoning to the root cause is as follows:

---

The crash is a result of over-eager uncaching combined with R-specific list
behaviors:

1.  **Over-eager Uncaching**: When `unloadNamespace("myTstS4")` runs, it calls
`methods::cacheMetaData(ns, FALSE, ns)` to clean up S4 metadata.
    *   Inside `cacheMetaData`, it loops over all generics the package has
methods for.
    *   If `attach = FALSE` (detaching/unloading), it calls `.uncacheGeneric(f,
fdef)` for **all** these generics, even if they belong to other packages (e.g.,
base generics like `Math` or `Ops` defined in `base`/`methods`).
    *   This completely deletes the generic (e.g., `Math`) from the `methods`
internal cache (`.genericTable`).

2.  **Cache Miss in Minimal Session**: Later, `.updateMethodsInTable` is called
to remove the package's methods from the generic.
    *   For a group generic (like `Math`), it loops over its members (e.g.,
`abs`).
    *   For each member, it calls `.updateMethodsInTable(abs_generic)`.
    *   Since `abs` belongs to group `Math`, it calls `getGeneric("Math")` to
verify signature lengths in `.checkGroupSigLength`.
    *   Since `Math` was just removed from the cache, and `methods` is not
attached (so it is not on the search path), `getGeneric("Math")` fails to find
it and silently returns `NULL`.

3.  **R List Assignment & `unlist()` Gotcha**:
    *   In `.updateMethodsInTable`, the list of generics is constructed:
        ```
        generics <- vector("list", length(groups))
        for(i in seq_along(groups))
            generics[[i]] <- getGeneric(groups[[i]])
        ```
    *   If `getGeneric` returns `NULL`, the assignment `generics[[i]] <- NULL`
**deletes** the element from the list, reducing the list's length (e.g., from 1
to 0).
    *   Inside `.checkGroupSigLength`, when `recall` is triggered, it calls
`Recall(unlist(gnames, FALSE), unlist(generics, FALSE))`.
    *   `unlist()` silently drops `NULL` elements from lists.
    *   This causes the `generics` list to become shorter than the `gnames`
list.
    *   In the recursive call, the loop `for(i in seq_along(gnames))` attempts
to access `generics[[i]]` where `i` is out of bounds, triggering the `subscript
out of bounds` crash.

text:
The fix restricts uncaching to only those generics that actually belong to the
package being unloaded.

---

Even if the suggested fix is found deficient, I hope this diagnosis points the
way to a fix.

This is tested on r-svn: <https://github.com/r-devel/r-svn/pull/258>

text:
*[Bug 19079] S4 packages with group methods error with "subscript out of bounds" under unloadNamespace with Imports: methods*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19079>

Michael Lawrence (<mailto:lawremi@gmail.com|lawremi@gmail.com>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |lawremi@gmail.com

--- Comment #1 from Michael Lawrence (<mailto:lawremi@gmail.com|lawremi@gmail.com>) ---
Thanks for the report and the suggested fix.

One thing to watch out for with S4 generics is that `@package` points to the
package defining the function that the generic represents. So for example if a
package does `setGeneric("nrow", ...)`, `@package` will be "base", and with
this fix, the `nrow()` methods would remain. 

Why not see what your friend does with that feedback :)

<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19079>

Michael Chirico (<mailto:michaelchirico4@gmail.com|michaelchirico4@gmail.com>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
   Attachment #3716|0                           |1
        is obsolete|                            |

--- Comment #2 from Michael Chirico (<mailto:michaelchirico4@gmail.com|michaelchirico4@gmail.com>) ---
Created attachment 3717
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3717&action=edit>
Updated patch for unloading generics without methods to handle e.g. base
generics better

Great point! I was content to just let the unload process only finish partially
in that case, but indeed our friend was able to push further by looking for the
definition in the correct environment.

Updated here and on GitHub with a new test case ensuring `nrow()` reverts to
non-generic after unloading the test package: `stopifnot(!isGeneric('nrow'))`
